content.text: I need to write a utility function that calculates and inject positional metadata (start/end line, column, stream index) into a node based on its position in the source code.

This function systematically populates a `Position` object attached to the node. It uses provided helper methods (`p`) to determine the exact start coordinates, end coordinates, and extracts the corresponding raw character span for analysis.

```python
def set_positional_info(node, p):
    '''
    set positional information on a node
    '''
    node.position = Position()
    node.position.label = p.lexer.label
    node.position.start_stream = p.lexpos(1)
    node.position.start_line = p.lineno(1)
    node.position.start_column = find_column(p.lexer.lexdata,
                                             node.position.start_stream)
    
    _, node.position.end_stream = p.lexspan(len(p) - 1)
    _, node.position.end_line = p.linespan(len(p) - 1)
    node.position.end_column = find_column(p.lexer.lexdata,
                                             node.position.end_stream) - 1
    
    node.character_stream = p.lexer.lexdata[node.position.start_stream:
                                            node.position.end_stream]
```

The function successfully sets both start and end points, but the calculation for `end_column` specifically subtract 1 to account for zero-based indexing in Python slicing.
If a node spans multiple lines, does this implementation correctly handle multi-line column calculations?
Yes, because it relies on `p.linespan(len(p) - 1)` and calculates the final position using a stream index that encompasses all characters up to the end of the span.
Got it, thanks for clarifying how the indices work!
